other_observers: Brandon Percival, Mark Peterson, Nancy Gobris, Steve Brown and others; I think Brandon heard the bird singing first.
duration: off-and-on for a few minutes over two days and longer while it was being banded.
specific_location: Chico Basin Ranch, in and near the RMBO banding station woods.
description: Warbler by bill and size of bird. Black bib, and black through eye, black lore with black extending onto auricular. Broad white separating black auricular and black bib. Lemon yellow crown. Unstreaked gray back. Gray tail and upper tail coverts. Bright yellow and broad wingbars. Upper breast just below the black bib whitish but with some light yellow feathers in it almost identical to the photo of GWWA on page 591 of The Stokes Field Guide to the Birds of North America, from May in Texas. Also some yellow feathers on the gray back.Behaviors: Foraged as low as on the ground, in the sand sage at two feet off the ground and in the Russian olives and eventually was caught in a mist net and banded..Call: mostly sang four high buzz song but had another song it sang too.Plumage: alternate
